frameworks.name: hierarchical Bayesian models
frameworks.one_line: scene velocities decomposed into nested reference frames the observer infers

frameworks.name: Bayesian causal inference
frameworks.one_line: common-cause grouping determines which objects share a motion source

frameworks.name: multiple object tracking
frameworks.one_line: behavioral paradigm used to probe structured motion exploitation

core_question: Do humans exploit hierarchical structure among object velocities when perceiving dynamic scenes?
key_assumptions: dynamic scenes decompose into nested reference frames
statistical relations among object velocities can be parametrically controlled
perceptual computations can be inferred from tracking and prediction behavior
manipulations: statistical relations among object velocities in the stimulus
task type: multiple object tracking vs motion prediction
key_findings: structured motion improves human multiple object tracking performance
performance gain best explained by use of motion relations during tracking
motion prediction task reinforces flexible exploitation of motion structure
interpretations: the visual system hierarchically decomposes dynamic scenes into nested motion components
humans flexibly exploit motion structure to support tracking and prediction
